message:
chore(beads): prune stale 20260312 history snapshots, add 20260313-14 snapshots

Remove five old .br_history snapshot pairs from 2026-03-12 that were
cleaned up during a beads database rebuild/recovery cycle. Add five
new snapshot pairs from 2026-03-13 and 2026-03-14. Update
issues.jsonl to reflect current issue state (snapshot-test task
closed, issue field updates).

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -99,7 +99,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-6q0","title":"Create deterministic snapshot tests for all TUI screens","description":"Use ftui-harness for golden-file snapshot testing of all 12 screens.

## What Is Snapshot Testing (ftui-harness)
FrankenTUI provides ftui-harness for deterministic UI testing:
- Render screen to a Buffer with known dimensions
- Compare against golden-file snapshot
- BLESS=1 cargo test to update golden files
- Deterministic: same input always produces same output

## What To Do
1. Add ftui-harness to vc_tui dev-dependencies
2. Create test module: #[cfg(test)] mod snapshot_tests
3. For each of the 12 screens:
   a. Create mock data that exercises all visual elements
   b. Render to a Buffer at fixed dimensions (e.g., 120x40)
   c. Compare against golden file in tests/snapshots/
   d. Include both empty state and populated state tests
4. Create golden files: BLESS=1 cargo test -p vc_tui

## Test Cases Per Screen
Each screen needs at minimum:
- Empty state (no data loaded yet)
- Normal state (representative data)
- Error state (if screen can show errors)
- Boundary state (very long values, many rows)

## Snapshot File Layout
tests/snapshots/
  overview_empty.snap
  overview_normal.snap
  machines_empty.snap
  machines_normal.snap
  ...etc for all 12 screens

## Files Changed
- crates/vc_tui/Cargo.toml (dev-dependency)
- crates/vc_tui/src/lib.rs (test module)
- crates/vc_tui/tests/snapshots/ (golden files)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-12T04:01:02.095116010Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-13T12:18:31.785597765Z","closed_at":"2026-03-13T12:18:31.784800445Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["snapshots","testing","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-6q0","depends_on_id":"bd-0di","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T04:06:13.624233081Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-6q0","depends_on_id":"bd-1l1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T04:06:20.356558251Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-6q0","depends_on_id":"bd-1l8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T04:06:15.837875467Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-6q0","depends_on_id":"bd-rq9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T04:06:18.044627656Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-6sr","title":"Fix schema mismatches: collector_health uses wrong column names (checked_at, collector_name)","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-22T01:21:11.667436118Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T01:21:26.868363125Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T01:21:26.867791053Z","close_reason":"Fixed: replaced checked_at→collected_at, collector_name→collector, status→success, records_collected→rows_inserted, error_message→error_class in vc_mcp, vc_web, vc_store, vc_query/nl.rs","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-7b7","title":"Implement cloud_benchmarker collector","description":"Implement collector for cloud_benchmarker - VPS machine performance baseline and drift detection.

INTEGRATION METHOD:
- GET /data/raw/ from its FastAPI server
- GET /data/overall/ for aggregate scores
- Or read SQLite directly if available
- Use CLI Snapshot or HTTP scrape pattern

DUCKDB TABLES:
CREATE TABLE cloud_bench_raw(
    machine_id TEXT,
    collected_at TIMESTAMP,
    benchmark_type TEXT,
    benchmark_name TEXT,
    value REAL,
    unit TEXT,
    raw_json TEXT,
    PRIMARY KEY (machine_id, collected_at, benchmark_type, benchmark_name)
);

CREATE TABLE cloud_bench_overall(
    machine_id TEXT,
    collected_at TIMESTAMP,
    overall_score REAL,
    cpu_score REAL,
    memory_score REAL,
    disk_score REAL,
    network_score REAL,
    subscores_json TEXT,
    raw_json TEXT,
    PRIMARY KEY (machine_id, collected_at)
);

CREATE TABLE cloud_bench_history(
    machine_id TEXT,
    benchmark_date DATE,
    overall_score REAL,
    delta_from_baseline REAL,
    anomaly_detected BOOLEAN,
    PRIMARY KEY (machine_id, benchmark_date)
);

COLLECTOR IMPLEMENTATION:
- Implement CloudBenchCollector with Collector trait
- Try HTTP endpoint first (if benchmarker server running)
- Fall back to SQLite direct read
- Calculate drift from baseline (first benchmark = baseline)
- Flag significant performance degradation (>10% from baseline)
- Handle missing benchmarker gracefully

UI VALUE:
- Machine performance baselines
- Performance drift alerts
- VPS provider comparison
- Capacity planning data

DRIFT DETECTION:
- Store first benchmark as baseline per machine
- Calculate delta on each collection
- Alert if delta > threshold (configurable, default 10%)
- Track trends over time

TESTS:
- Mock FastAPI responses
- Test SQLite fallback
- Verify drift calculation

## E2E & Logging
- Add/extend `tests/e2e/collectors/test_<collector>.sh` (see bd-30z).
- Emit structured logs: collector, machine_id, duration_ms, rows_inserted, stderr.
- Write JSON summary artifact to `tests/logs/`.

-{"id":"bd-880","title":"Remove ratatui and crossterm workspace dependencies","description":"Final cleanup: remove all ratatui and crossterm references from workspace.

## What To Do
1. Remove ratatui from workspace Cargo.toml [workspace.dependencies]
2. Remove crossterm from workspace Cargo.toml [workspace.dependencies]
3. Remove both from crates/vc_tui/Cargo.toml
4. Search for any remaining 'use ratatui::' or 'use crossterm::' and remove
5. Run cargo check --workspace to verify clean build

## Verification
- grep -r 'ratatui' should return zero results (excluding .git, target)
- grep -r 'crossterm' should return zero results (excluding .git, target)
- Cargo.lock should no longer contain ratatui or crossterm entries

## Testing (8 verification checks)

### Pre-Removal Audit (2 checks)
1. check_no_ratatui_imports — grep -r 'use ratatui::' across all crates returns zero matches (screen port beads should have removed all usage)
2. check_no_crossterm_imports — grep -r 'use crossterm::' across all crates returns zero matches

### Post-Removal Build Verification (3 checks)
3. test_cargo_check_workspace — `cargo check --workspace` succeeds with exit code 0, no unresolved import errors
4. test_cargo_test_vc_tui — `cargo test -p vc_tui` passes all 142 tests (no regressions from dependency removal)
5. test_cargo_test_workspace — `cargo test --workspace` passes (no transitive breakage in other crates)

### Dependency Tree Verification (3 checks)
6. check_cargo_lock_no_ratatui — `grep -c 'name = "ratatui"' Cargo.lock` returns 0
7. check_cargo_lock_no_crossterm — `grep -c 'name = "crossterm"' Cargo.lock` returns 0
8. check_cargo_tree_clean — `cargo tree -p vc_tui` shows ftui as TUI dependency, no ratatui/crossterm in tree

### Logging
- Log each removal step with file path and line removed
- Log grep audit results with match counts
- Log cargo check/test output summary (pass count, duration)
- If any grep matches found pre-removal, log them as errors with file:line for manual review

## Files Changed
- Cargo.toml (workspace root)
- crates/vc_tui/Cargo.toml
- Cargo.lock (regenerated)","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-12T04:01:01.532949451Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-13T22:31:12.088073533Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cargo","cleanup","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-880","depends_on_id":"bd-0di","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T04:06:04.969116468Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-880","depends_on_id":"bd-1l1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T04:06:11.430739702Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-880","depends_on_id":"bd-1l8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T04:06:07.102209782Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-880","depends_on_id":"bd-rq9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T04:06:09.250728181Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-9tig","title":"Verify tests in indirect DuckDB consumer crates after migration","description":"After the DuckDB-to-FrankenSQLite migration, verify that tests in crates that indirectly consume VcStore still pass. These crates don't reference duckdb directly but depend on vc_store or vc_query.

## Crates to Verify

### 1. vc_web (HIGHEST RISK)
- 54 tests using in-memory DuckDB via VcStore
- **Critical**: 13 REST endpoints use raw SQL via store.query_json() BYPASSING vc_query
  (These queries are ported separately in bd-s8lm, but tests here must validate the results)
- **Boolean format risk**: Frontend TypeScript expects `acknowledged: boolean` (true/false)
  but SQLite stores 0/1 as INTEGER. JSON serialization must convert 0→false, 1→true.
  Verify: serde_json serialization of SqliteValue::Integer for boolean columns.
- **Timestamp format risk**: Frontend parses ISO 8601 strings. DuckDB TIMESTAMP → SQLite TEXT
  with datetime('now') format. Verify strftime output matches frontend expectations.
- **Metrics endpoint**: Hardcoded SQL queries for COUNT(*), AVG(), SUM() aggregations.
  SQLite aggregation semantics match but type coercion may differ (e.g., AVG returns REAL).
- Files: crates/vc_web/src/lib.rs

### 2. vc_alert
- Alert threshold evaluation tests
- Risk: If query return types change (e.g., Option wrapping), alert logic breaks
- Files: crates/vc_alert/src/lib.rs

### 3. vc_cli
- Command execution tests
- Risk: Error type changes propagating through clap handlers
- Files: crates/vc_cli/src/lib.rs

### 4. vc_mcp
- MCP tool call tests
- Risk: JSON serialization of query results may differ
- Files: crates/vc_mcp/src/lib.rs

### 5. vc_oracle
- Prediction calculation tests
- Risk: Window function results may differ slightly between DuckDB and SQLite
- Files: crates/vc_oracle/src/lib.rs

### 6. vc_guardian
- Guardian decision tests
- Risk: Audit event insertion format changes
- Files: crates/vc_guardian/src/lib.rs

### 7. vc_collect
- Collector execution tests
- Risk: insert_json parameter format changes
- Files: crates/vc_collect/src/lib.rs

## Verification Procedure
1. Run `cargo test -p <crate>` for each crate individually after migration completes
2. Fix any compilation errors from API changes (StoreError, Connection type)
3. Fix any test failures from behavioral differences
4. **For vc_web specifically**: Add format-assertion tests:
   - Assert boolean fields serialize as true/false (not 0/1) in JSON responses
   - Assert timestamp fields are valid ISO 8601 in JSON responses
   - Assert null fields serialize as JSON null (not missing)
5. Log results: crate name, test count, pass/fail, fix applied

## Success Criteria
- All existing tests pass across all 7 indirect consumer crates
- No compilation errors related to database types
- vc_web JSON responses match frontend TypeScript expectations
- Test execution logged with per-crate results

## Files Changed
- Potentially minor fixes in test setup code across all 7 crates
- vc_web may need serde helper for boolean 0/1→true/false conversion","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-12T05:37:25.564815528Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T15:30:34.744527037Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["testing","validation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-9tig","depends_on_id":"bd-a38","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T05:37:32.746071668Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-9tig","depends_on_id":"bd-kft","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T05:37:33.698515786Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-9tig","depends_on_id":"bd-s8lm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T15:30:34.743830434Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-9us","title":"Build data migration tool (DuckDB to FrankenSQLite)","description":"CLI command to migrate existing DuckDB data to FrankenSQLite format.

## What To Do
1. Add 'vc migrate-db' subcommand to vc_cli
2. Accept --from <path.duckdb> --to <path.sqlite> arguments
3. Implementation strategy:
   a. Open source DuckDB file (requires duckdb dep — must run BEFORE bd-a38 removes it)
   b. Create target FrankenSQLite database with all migrations applied
   c. For each table: SELECT all rows from DuckDB, INSERT into FrankenSQLite
   d. Handle type conversions during transfer:
      - BOOLEAN true/false → INTEGER 1/0
      - TIMESTAMP → TEXT (ISO 8601)
      - TEXT[] / DOUBLE[] → JSON TEXT arrays
      - LIST/MAP/STRUCT → JSON TEXT
   e. Verify row counts match after transfer
   f. Report progress per table

## Alternative Approach (if duckdb dep already removed)
- Export from DuckDB using external duckdb CLI: duckdb <file> -csv "SELECT * FROM <table>"
- Import into FrankenSQLite from CSV
- Provide shell script wrapper: vc-migrate-data.sh
- This approach is less reliable (shell parsing, escaping) but avoids Rust dep

## Progress Reporting
- Log each table being migrated
- Show row count: 'Migrating agent_sessions: 15432 rows... done (2.3s)'
- Show type conversions applied
- Final summary: total tables, total rows migrated, total time, any errors

## Integrity Verification (built into tool)
After migration, automatically verify:
- Row counts match per table (source vs target)
- Sample data spot-checks (first/last/3 random rows per table)
- NULL handling preserved (count NULLs per column matches)

## Testing
- Unit test: type conversion BOOLEAN true → INTEGER 1
- Unit test: type conversion TIMESTAMP → TEXT ISO 8601
- Unit test: type conversion TEXT[] → JSON array
- Unit test: type conversion DOUBLE[] → JSON array
- Unit test: type conversion LIST/MAP/STRUCT → JSON
- Integration test: create DuckDB with 3 tables, 100 rows each, migrate, verify
- Integration test: migrate empty DuckDB (0 rows) — verify clean success
- Integration test: migrate with all NULL columns — verify NULLs preserved
- Integration test: migrate with Unicode data — verify encoding preserved
- Integration test: verify row counts match after migration
- Test progress logging output format
- Test --from with non-existent file — verify clear error message
- Test --to with existing file — verify warning/overwrite behavior
- Log: source table, target table, row count, type conversions, duration

## Files Changed
- crates/vc_cli/src/lib.rs (new subcommand)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:59:08.764471218Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-13T21:12:23.410018400Z","closed_at":"2026-03-13T21:12:23.409356623Z","close_reason":"Implemented vc migrate-db with data conversion and verification","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["database","migration","tooling"]}
 {"id":"bd-a38","title":"Remove duckdb from workspace dependencies entirely","description":"Final cleanup: remove all duckdb references from the workspace.

## What To Do
1. Remove 'duckdb = { version = "1.4", features = ["bundled"] }' from workspace Cargo.toml
2. Remove duckdb.workspace = true from crates/vc_store/Cargo.toml
3. Remove duckdb.workspace = true from crates/vc_knowledge/Cargo.toml (if present)
4. Search for any remaining 'use duckdb' statements and remove
5. Search for any 'duckdb::' qualified paths and remove
6. Run cargo check --workspace to verify clean build
7. Run cargo clippy --workspace to verify no warnings

## Verification
- grep -r 'duckdb' across entire workspace should return zero results
- Cargo.lock should no longer contain duckdb or any duckdb transitive deps
- Build should succeed without downloading/compiling duckdb

## Why This Is a Separate Bead
The duckdb dependency is kept temporarily while porting to allow gradual migration.
This bead is the final 'flip the switch' that removes it entirely.
All port beads must be complete before this runs.

## Build Impact
Removing duckdb (bundled C compilation) should dramatically reduce:
- Build time (no more C compilation)
- Memory usage during build (no more OOM on workers)
- Binary size (no embedded DuckDB engine)

## Files Changed
- Cargo.toml (workspace root)
- crates/vc_store/Cargo.toml
- crates/vc_knowledge/Cargo.toml
- Cargo.lock (regenerated)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:59:07.189589320Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T17:08:24.797159109Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cargo","cleanup","database"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-a38","depends_on_id":"bd-0q1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T04:05:17.700675601Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-a38","depends_on_id":"bd-30c","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T04:05:19.177399974Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-a38","depends_on_id":"bd-9us","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T17:08:24.793019392Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-a38","depends_on_id":"bd-bql","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T04:05:16.236535625Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-a38","depends_on_id":"bd-kft","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T04:05:22.212732051Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-a38","depends_on_id":"bd-ute","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T04:05:20.710398730Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
@@ -115,10 +115,10 @@
 {"id":"bd-f2q","title":"E2E test: concurrent collector writes with MVCC verification","description":"Test that MVCC concurrent writers handle parallel collector inserts correctly.

## Test Scenario
1. Create FrankenSQLite store with concurrent_mode = ON
2. Spawn 14 threads (one per collector type)
3. Each thread:
   a. Opens its own connection to the same database
   b. Begins CONCURRENT transaction
   c. Inserts 100 rows into its collector table
   d. Commits (with retry on BUSY_SNAPSHOT)
4. Wait for all threads to complete
5. Verify:
   - All 1400 rows present (14 * 100)
   - No data corruption or missing rows
   - Conflict stats show reasonable conflict rate
   - All transactions eventually committed

## Conflict Stress Test
Additionally test high-conflict scenario:
- 8 threads all inserting into the SAME table
- Verify eventual convergence
- Log retry counts per thread
- Verify total conflict rate

## Performance Metrics
- Time to complete all inserts
- Average retries per thread
- Peak conflict rate
- Comparison: sequential vs concurrent throughput

## Logging Requirements
- Per-thread: insert count, retry count, total time
- Global: total rows, total conflicts, total time
- Summary table at end

 {"id":"bd-f9a","title":"MVCC concurrent writer tests","description":"Comprehensive tests for FrankenSQLite MVCC concurrent write behavior.

## Test Scenarios

### 1. Parallel collector inserts (no conflict)
- Spawn 4 threads, each inserting into different tables (different pages)
- All should commit successfully with zero BUSY_SNAPSHOT
- Verify all data present after all commits

### 2. Conflict detection and retry
- Two threads insert into same table, same page range
- At least one should get BUSY_SNAPSHOT
- Retry logic should eventually succeed
- All data present after retries

### 3. Read consistency during writes
- Writer thread continuously inserting
- Reader thread continuously querying
- Reader should never see partial transactions
- Snapshot isolation: reader sees consistent point-in-time view

### 4. Connection pool stress test
- Create pool of 8 connections
- 14 concurrent tasks each acquiring a connection
- Each performs insert + query cycle
- Verify no data loss or corruption

### 5. Transaction advisor verification
- Run workload that triggers each advisor warning:
  - long_txn (hold transaction open > threshold)
  - large_read_set (scan many pages)
  - rollback_pressure (many retries)
- Verify advisor output is correct

### 6. Conflict statistics
- Run concurrent writes
- Query PRAGMA fsqlite.conflict_stats
- Verify counters increment correctly

## Logging
Each test should log:
- Thread ID and operation
- Transaction begin/commit/rollback timestamps
- Conflict count per thread
- Total execution time

-{"id":"bd-fej","title":"Adopt Outcome type for collector and guardian operations","description":"Replace Result with asupersync Outcome where cancellation semantics matter.

## What Is Outcome
Asupersync Outcome<T, E> is a four-valued result:
- Ok(T) — normal completion
- Err(E) — error during work
- Cancelled(reason) — cancelled and drained successfully
- Panicked(payload) — panic during work

This is richer than Result<T, E> because it distinguishes:
- 'Failed' (retry might help) from 'Cancelled' (intentional, don't retry)
- 'Cancelled cleanly' from 'Panicked' (unexpected, escalate)

## Where To Adopt

### Collector Operations
Current: Result<CollectResult, CollectError>
Target: Outcome<CollectResult, CollectError>

When a collector is cancelled mid-operation:
- Return Cancelled(reason) instead of Err(Timeout)
- Caller knows not to retry (it was intentional shutdown)

### Guardian Playbook Execution
Current: Result<PlaybookRun, GuardianError>
Target: Outcome<PlaybookRun, GuardianError>

When a playbook is cancelled:
- Record RunStatus::Aborted with cancellation reason
- Don't count against max_runs_per_hour

### Alert Delivery
Current: Result<(), AlertError>
Target: Outcome<(), AlertError>

When delivery is cancelled during shutdown:
- Don't retry immediately
- Queue for next startup

## Migration Pattern
- fn collect() -> Result<R, E> becomes fn collect() -> Outcome<R, E>
- match result { Ok(r) => ..., Err(e) => ... }
  becomes match result { Ok(r) => ..., Err(e) => ..., Cancelled(r) => ..., Panicked(p) => ... }

## Testing
- Unit test: Outcome::Ok matches correctly, extracts value
- Unit test: Outcome::Err matches correctly, extracts error
- Unit test: Outcome::Cancelled matches correctly, extracts reason
- Unit test: Outcome::Panicked matches correctly, extracts payload
- Unit test: collector returns Cancelled on cx.checkpoint() cancel — verify caller doesn't retry
- Unit test: guardian returns Cancelled — verify not counted against max_runs_per_hour
- Unit test: alert returns Cancelled — verify queued for next startup
- Integration test: cancel collector fleet mid-run — all return Cancelled, not Err
- Verify exhaustive match: compiler enforces all 4 variants handled at every call site
- Verify Outcome<T,E> works with ? operator (Ok propagates, others handled explicitly)
- Log: operation name, outcome variant, reason/error details

 {"id":"bd-gdc","title":"Epic: Deep Asupersync Integration","description":"Go beyond shallow asupersync integration to leverage structured concurrency, deterministic testing, and capability-based security throughout the vibe_cockpit codebase.

## Current State (Shallow Integration)
Today, asupersync is used only as:
- RuntimeBuilder::new().build() in main.rs
- Cx::for_request() root context
- with_tokio_context() compat bridge
- Basic async/await (futures resolution)
- sleep/timeout/interval with asupersync API

## What Is NOT Used (Deep Features)
- Structured concurrency (regions, scopes, scope.spawn())
- Cancellation checkpoints (cx.checkpoint())
- Two-phase effects (channel reserve/commit)
- Outcome type (Ok/Err/Cancelled/Panicked four-valued result)
- Lab runtime (deterministic scheduling, virtual time, seed-based reproduction)
- Cx-aware synchronization (Mutex::lock(&cx), RwLock, Semaphore)
- Macaroon capability attenuation (time/scope/rate limits)
- Evidence sink (decision tracing)
- Backpressure via Semaphores

## Goal: "Correct by Structure, Not by Discipline"
Move from relying on developer discipline to having the type system and runtime enforce:
- No orphaned collectors on shutdown (region close guarantees)
- Bounded shutdown time (budget deadlines)
- Cancel-safe database transactions (two-phase)
- Deterministic reproduction of concurrency bugs (lab runtime)
- Fine-grained authorization (Macaroon tokens)
- Observable drain progress (spectral monitoring)

## Scope
- Wrap collector fleet in structured regions with scope.spawn()
- Add cx.checkpoint() at collector lifecycle boundaries
- Implement graceful shutdown with bounded drain budgets
- Migrate std::sync::Mutex → asupersync Mutex (Cx-aware) where appropriate
- Add Semaphore-based backpressure for collector throughput
- Implement two-phase effects for database writes
- Create LabRuntime test harness for deterministic concurrency testing
- Add Macaroon capability attenuation for internal authorization
- Integrate evidence sink with audit trail
- Adopt Outcome type where cancellation semantics matter

## Success Criteria
- All collectors wrapped in structured regions
- Graceful shutdown completes within bounded time
- Lab tests reproduce concurrency scenarios deterministically
- No std::sync::Mutex in async code paths (replaced with Cx-aware)
- Semaphore limits concurrent collector count
- Evidence trail captures guardian/autopilot decisions","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:54:54.231275270Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T04:11:37.278686619Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","asupersync","concurrency"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-gdc","depends_on_id":"bd-1gk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T04:10:42.117918366Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-gdc","depends_on_id":"bd-46p","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T04:10:28.813205639Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-gdc","depends_on_id":"bd-4mb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T04:11:09.156473892Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-gdc","depends_on_id":"bd-66h","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T04:10:15.050932561Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-gdc","depends_on_id":"bd-fej","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T04:10:55.805510106Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-gdc","depends_on_id":"bd-m76","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T04:11:22.484894569Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-gdc","depends_on_id":"bd-qdp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T04:10:01.117588700Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-gdc","depends_on_id":"bd-zo8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T04:11:37.277234921Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-h0l","title":"Add cancellation checkpoints to collector lifecycle","description":"Insert explicit cx.checkpoint() calls at collector lifecycle boundaries.

## What Are Checkpoints
cx.checkpoint() is an explicit cancellation observation point. When called:
- If cancellation has been requested, the task enters drain phase
- If no cancellation, continues normally (zero-cost check)
- This prevents long-running operations from ignoring cancel signals

## Where To Add Checkpoints
For each collector's collect() method:

1. Before SSH connect: cx.checkpoint()?;
   -> Avoids initiating new SSH connection if shutting down

2. After SSH connect, before command execution: cx.checkpoint()?;
   -> Avoids running command if shutting down (connection will be cleaned up)

3. After command execution, before parsing: cx.checkpoint()?;
   -> Avoids parsing if shutting down (raw output discarded)

4. After parsing, before database insert: cx.checkpoint()?;
   -> Avoids partial inserts if shutting down

5. After database insert: cx.checkpoint()?;
   -> Confirms successful completion or drains

## Drain Protocol for SSH Connections
When a checkpoint triggers cancellation during SSH operation:
- Close SSH channel gracefully
- Release connection back to pool
- Return Outcome::Cancelled(reason) instead of Err

## What To Do
1. Update Collector trait return type: Result<CollectResult> -> Outcome<CollectResult, CollectError>
2. Add checkpoint() calls at each lifecycle boundary
3. Implement drain cleanup for SSH connections
4. Log checkpoint hits: tracing::debug!(checkpoint = "pre_ssh_connect")

## Testing
- Unit test: mock collector with checkpoints, no cancellation — runs to completion
- Unit test: cancel before SSH connect checkpoint — verify no connection attempted
- Unit test: cancel after SSH connect, before command — verify connection closed cleanly
- Unit test: cancel after command, before parse — verify raw output discarded
- Unit test: cancel after parse, before insert — verify no partial data in store
- Unit test: cancel after insert — verify clean Outcome::Cancelled return
- Integration test: run collector in LabRuntime, trigger cancel at each checkpoint in sequence
- Verify tracing logs emit checkpoint names at each boundary
- Test Outcome::Cancelled propagates correctly to region (not treated as error)
- Log: checkpoint name, cancellation status, cleanup action taken

 {"id":"bd-h6y","title":"Translate migrations 019-026 to SQLite-compatible SQL","description":"Port migrations 19-26 from DuckDB syntax to SQLite syntax.

## Migrations to Port (121 lines total, low complexity)
19. 019_incident_replay.sql (11 lines) — Incident replay snapshot cache, 1 index
20. 020_alert_routing.sql (14 lines) — Alert routing decision events, 3 indexes
21. 021_adaptive_polling.sql (27 lines) — Adaptive polling intervals with 2 tables, 3 indexes
22. 022_node_ingest.sql (17 lines) — Data ingestion tracking, 3 indexes
23. 023_redaction_events.sql (16 lines) — Redaction event audit log, 2 indexes
24. 024_digest_reports.sql (14 lines) — Digest report storage, 2 indexes
25. 025_collector_tables.sql (83 lines) — Additional collector output tables (4 tables), 6 indexes
    - Largest in batch, but straightforward TEXT/TIMESTAMP/INTEGER columns
26. 026_machine_status.sql (6 lines) — ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN to machines

## Translation Rules (same as bd-dfl)
- TIMESTAMP -> TEXT (ISO8601 strings)
- BOOLEAN -> INTEGER (0/1)
- BIGINT -> INTEGER
- REAL -> REAL (same)
- current_timestamp -> datetime('now')

## Special Considerations
- 025 is the largest file (83 lines) with 4 new tables for collector data
  - Simple schemas: TEXT, TIMESTAMP, INTEGER, REAL, BOOLEAN
  - 6 indexes — standard CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS
- 026 uses ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN on machines table (from migration 001)
- No array types, no ENUM types, no complex types in this batch
- No foreign keys in this batch
- 14 CREATE INDEX statements total — all use IF NOT EXISTS
- This is the simplest batch to translate

## Testing
After translation, verify:
1. Each migration file parses in fsqlite
2. Tables created with correct column types
3. ALTER TABLE in 026 applies cleanly
4. All 14 indexes created successfully
5. INSERT test data accepted for 025's collector tables

 {"id":"bd-h8l","title":"Implement Elm Model trait for vc_tui App","description":"Restructure vc_tui App to implement FrankenTUI Elm Model trait.

## Current Architecture (lib.rs, 429 lines)
Imperative render loop:
1. App struct holds current_screen + per-screen data fields
2. Main loop: poll events -> handle_key() -> render()
3. render() dispatches to per-screen render function
4. IMPORTANT: Only 5 of 13 screens are wired in render dispatch (Overview, Accounts, Sessions, Mail, Oracle)
   The remaining 8 (Machines, Repos, Alerts, Guardian, Events, Beads, RCH, Settings) show placeholders
   despite having full implementations in screens/*.rs (487-820 lines each)
5. Key handling: crossterm KeyEvent matching

## Target Architecture (Elm/Bubbletea)
Implement ftui::Model trait — this fixes the missing screen dispatch:

### 1. Define AppMessage enum:
- Key(ftui::KeyEvent) — keyboard input
- Tick — periodic data refresh
- DataRefreshed(ScreenData) — new data arrived from store
- Error(String) — error occurred
- ScreenChanged(Screen) — navigation
- Quit — exit

### 2. Implement Model::init():
- Set initial screen to Overview
- Return Cmd to load initial data
- Start poll subscription

### 3. Implement Model::update(msg):
- Key events: screen navigation (Tab, number keys, letter shortcuts)
- Per-screen key handling delegated to screen modules
- Tick: trigger data refresh command
- DataRefreshed: update appropriate screen data field
- Quit: return Cmd::Quit

### 4. Implement Model::view(&self, frame):
- Create dispatch STUBS for all 13 screens — each stub renders placeholder using ftui widgets
- Screen port beads (bd-0di, bd-1l8, bd-rq9, bd-1l1) will replace stubs with full implementations
- This approach ensures bd-h8l compiles independently without waiting for all screen ports

### 5. Implement Model::subscriptions():
- Every(poll_interval) -> Tick message for dashboard refresh

## App Struct Changes
Current App struct has data fields for only 5 screens:
  overview_data, accounts_data, sessions_data, mail_data, oracle_data
Must add missing data fields:
  machines_data, alerts_data, guardian_data, events_data,
  beads_data, rch_data, settings_data

## Cmd Enum Usage
- Cmd::None — no side effect
- Cmd::Quit — exit app
- Cmd::Msg(AppMessage) — schedule message
- Cmd::Task(future) — async data fetch
- Cmd::Batch([...]) — multiple commands

## Screen Enum (13 variants)
Overview, Machines, Repos, Accounts, Sessions, Mail, Alerts,
Guardian, Oracle, Events, Beads, RCH, Settings
(+ Help as implicit 14th)
All have shortcut keys: o/m/r/a/s/l/!/g/p/e/b

## Testing
- Unit test: Model::init() returns correct initial state (Overview screen, empty data)
- Unit test: Model::init() returns Cmd to load initial data
- Unit test: update(Key('1')) -> screen changes to Overview
- Unit test: update(Key('2')) -> screen changes to Machines
- Unit test: update(Key(Tab)) -> screen cycles forward
- Unit test: update(Tick) -> returns Cmd::Task for data refresh
- Unit test: update(DataRefreshed(data)) -> screen data updated
- Unit test: update(Quit) -> returns Cmd::Quit
- Unit test: view() dispatches to correct screen stub for each Screen variant
- Unit test: subscriptions() returns tick subscription with correct interval
- Snapshot test: render each screen stub to Buffer — verify placeholder content
- Log: message type, state before/after, Cmd returned

 {"id":"bd-hdic","title":"Data integrity verification for DuckDB-to-FrankenSQLite migration","description":"Verify that data migrated from DuckDB to FrankenSQLite is identical.

## Test Scenario
1. Create a DuckDB database with representative data:
   - All 26 migrations applied
   - 1000 rows in key tables (system_metrics, agent_sessions, etc.)
   - Known data patterns for verification
2. Run migration tool (from data migration bead)
3. Verify integrity:

### Row Count Verification
For each of ~60 tables:
- Query COUNT(*) in both DuckDB source and FrankenSQLite target
- Assert counts match exactly
- Log any discrepancies

### Data Value Verification
For key tables, spot-check specific rows:
- Query first row, last row, and 10 random rows
- Compare all column values
- Handle type differences (BOOLEAN -> INTEGER, TIMESTAMP -> TEXT)

### JSON Column Verification
For tables with JSON columns (LIST/MAP/STRUCT in DuckDB):
- Parse JSON in both databases
- Compare parsed values (not raw strings, as formatting may differ)

### NULL Handling
- Verify NULL values preserved correctly
- Check that empty strings are not converted to NULLs or vice versa

### Checksum Verification
For each table:
- Compute a checksum over all rows (sorted by primary key)
- Compare checksums between source and target

## Logging
- Per-table: row count, checksum, sample values, pass/fail
- Summary: tables checked, tables passed, tables failed
- On failure: show first mismatched row with expected vs actual
